content: Water retention occurs when sodium chloride, a liquid solution, creates an isotonic state in the body, causing water to be retained in tissues and under the skin.
Dandelion root extract increases urine production, leading to a 5% decrease in fluid retention according to a 2009 study in the Journal of Alternative and Complementary Medicine.
Magnesium helps balance sodium and potassium levels, with magnesium deficiency being common among Americans who consume an average of 3.5 grams of sodium daily.
Exercise, particularly active movements like swimming, mechanically moves fluid out of tissues and into the bloodstream, aiding kidney function and fluid excretion.

Walking reduces fluid retention more effectively than hanging in the Trendelenburg position, with a 30% greater reduction in fluid retention observed in walkers compared to those in the Trendelenburg position.
